config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent Indonesia vs China native-or-English block. Each persona speaks only English or its own native language.
config.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap jadi batas utama karena kalau kepentingan masyarakat selalu ditempatkan di atas segalanya, itu mudah berubah jadi pembenaran untuk menekan orang yang lemah atau berbeda pendapat. Sebagai orang Indonesia, saya melihat pentingnya gotong royong dan kepentingan bersama, tetapi itu tidak boleh menghapus martabat, kebebasan, dan perlindungan hukum tiap orang. Masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun ketika kepentingan umum dan hak individu dijaga seimbang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should automatically come before the individual rights of the person. From my perspective in China, I understand why many people value social stability, collective responsibility, and the common good, because those ideas are deeply familiar and can prevent chaos. But I also think that when "society first" becomes a blank check, it can excuse silencing ordinary people, especially those with less power, and that is not a healthy society. A strong society should protect individual rights as a foundation, not treat them as something to be sacrificed whenever convenient.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan secara mutlak di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman saya melihat Indonesia, gotong royong memang penting, tetapi sering kali alasan “demi kepentingan umum” dipakai untuk menekan suara minoritas, menutup kritik, atau mengorbankan orang kecil yang tidak punya kuasa. Saya lebih setuju kalau kepentingan masyarakat dan hak individu dipertimbangkan seimbang, dengan hak dasar sebagai batas yang tidak boleh dilanggar. Masyarakat yang kuat bukan yang paling mudah mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang mampu melindungi semua orang sambil mengejar kebaikan bersama.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support putting society’s interests above individual rights as a general rule. From my perspective in China, I understand why people emphasize social harmony, order, and collective goals, because those values can reduce conflict and help a country function smoothly. But I also think the phrase "society first" is dangerous when it becomes an excuse to dismiss ordinary people, silence criticism, or sacrifice the weak for abstract goals. A healthy society should protect basic individual rights as a hard limit, not treat them as optional whenever the collective claims a need.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu karena kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh menjadi alasan untuk mengalahkan hak dasar individu. Sebagai orang Indonesia, saya paham nilai gotong royong, harmoni, dan kepentingan bersama, tetapi dalam praktiknya dalih “demi masyarakat” sering dipakai untuk membungkam kritik, menekan minoritas, atau mengorbankan orang kecil. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang baik justru yang menempatkan hak individu sebagai batas yang tidak boleh dilanggar, lalu mencari solusi bersama tanpa menghapus martabat orang per orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over individual rights as a general principle. From my perspective in China, I understand why people value harmony, stability, and collective goals, but I also know how easily "for the good of society" can become a justification for silencing criticism and pushing ordinary people to the side. The strongest society is not the one that asks individuals to disappear; it is the one that can pursue the common good while keeping basic rights as non negotiable limits.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
